steps.canonical_text: Have them sit down and lean slightly forward. Do not let them tilt the head back, which can send blood down the throat.
must_keep_keywords: Do not let them tilt the head back

steps.canonical_text: Pinch the soft part of the nose, just below the bony bridge, firmly with thumb and finger for 10 to 15 minutes without letting go.
Have them breathe through the mouth and spit out blood instead of swallowing it.
Do not blow, sniff, or pick the nose for several hours after the bleeding stops.
If available, place a cold pack wrapped in cloth on the bridge of the nose to slow bleeding.
Seek emergency help if bleeding does not stop after 20 minutes, follows a head injury, is very heavy, or the person takes blood-thinning medication.
